items.title: Your agent's memory should compute confidence, not store it
items.description:
Most agent memory stores a confidence score the way it stores everything else. You
write it once and it sits there. The agent decides a fact is worth 0.9, the store
keeps 0.9, and three weeks later, after something has contradicted that fact, the
store still hands back 0.9. Confidence

items.description:

items.title: PWA with React + Leaflet: lessons from a 31-city map app
items.description:
Notes from shipping a real-world PWA in Korea:

  1. Vite SSG is worth it. SPAs lose Korean SEO because Naver's crawler doesn't fully render JS.
  2. Leaflet > Mapbox for cost-sensitive projects. Free OSM tiles + zero cost.
  3. Firebase Hosting rewrites let you have deep URLs (

items.title: Why Debugging Is Changing Forever: How AI Is Transforming the Way Developers Fix Software
items.description:
